subject: a young male elf scrambling backward on wooden floorboards, thrusting a brilliantly glowing tambourine forward as an improvised weapon against an unseen threat
description: A young Black male elf with pointed ears and a bright magenta headband sits low on worn wooden floorboards, weight braced on his left hand as he thrusts a glowing tambourine outward with his right. The tambourine's drumhead blazes warm gold — the single hottest light source in the frame — and its jingles catch the glow. He wears a green jacket over a pink-magenta sash, pale trousers, and dark boots; a small device clipped to his belt emits a green light. His face is turned upward and to the right, mouth open in a shout or gasp, eyes wide with fear. Around him the interior of what reads as a ruined house is overtaken by nature: dense blue-green vines and leafy vegetation press in from all sides, with a large broken window at upper left letting in cool moonlit blue-teal light. Dozens of small white moth-like creatures drift through the air. The floorboards beneath his legs take on a cyan-blue glow. The composition is frozen at a moment of desperate defensive action, the tambourine held aloft as the figure's last resort.
foreground: elf figure sprawled on wooden floorboards, raising a glowing tambourine with right hand, left hand splayed for support
background: ruined interior overtaken by dense blue-green vines and vegetation, moonlit broken window upper-left, white moths drifting through the air
emotion: wide-eyed terror, mouth open in a shout or gasp, body pressed low and back in a desperate defensive lean
